articles.title: AEP, other Ohio utilities could own nuclear power plants under state bill
articles.summary: A new bill in Ohio's House of Representatives could allow utilities like American Electric Power and FirstEnergy to own nuclear power plants, a practice generally barred in the state. While AEP is exploring small modular nuclear projects due to load growth, the Ohio Manufacturers’ Association opposes the bill, arguing it reintroduces financial risks to customers similar to a past bribery scandal. The legislation outlines a regulatory framework for financing and ratemaking, requiring customer agreements and PUC approval based on energy independence and supply needs.
articles.content: A new bill in Ohio's House of Representatives could allow utilities like American Electric Power and FirstEnergy to own nuclear power plants, a practice generally barred in the state. While AEP is exploring small modular nuclear projects due to load growth, the Ohio Manufacturers’ Association opposes the bill, arguing it reintroduces financial risks to customers similar to a past bribery scandal. The legislation outlines a regulatory framework for financing and ratemaking, requiring customer agreements and PUC approval based on energy independence and supply needs.

articles.title: Wall Street and crypto are merging again as OKX rolls out traditional oil benchmarks
articles.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), owner of the New York Stock Exchange, and crypto exchange OKX are collaborating to launch perpetual oil futures based on ICE’s Brent and WTI benchmark prices. This partnership will provide OKX's 120 million retail traders access to regulated energy products, highlighting the increasing convergence of traditional finance and crypto. The move comes as regulators plan to oversee perpetual futures and follows the success of similar non-expiring oil futures offered by Hyperliquid.
articles.content: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), owner of the New York Stock Exchange, and crypto exchange OKX are collaborating to launch perpetual oil futures based on ICE’s Brent and WTI benchmark prices. This partnership will provide OKX's 120 million retail traders access to regulated energy products, highlighting the increasing convergence of traditional finance and crypto. The move comes as regulators plan to oversee perpetual futures and follows the success of similar non-expiring oil futures offered by Hyperliquid.

articles.title: Verizon (VZ), AT&T, and T-Mobile Form A Joint Venture to End Mobile Dead Zones Across the US
articles.summary: Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile have formed a joint venture to eliminate mobile dead zones across the U.S., particularly in rural areas. This initiative will leverage satellite-based "direct to device" technology for broader connectivity and improved network reliability during natural disasters. Analysts suggest this move could be partly defensive, anticipating future competition from companies like SpaceX in the wireless market.
articles.content: Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile have formed a joint venture to eliminate mobile dead zones across the U.S., particularly in rural areas. This initiative will leverage satellite-based "direct to device" technology for broader connectivity and improved network reliability during natural disasters. Analysts suggest this move could be partly defensive, anticipating future competition from companies like SpaceX in the wireless market.

articles.title: T-Mobile (TMUS) Is Becoming an Account-Economics Story
articles.summary: T-Mobile's growth is shifting from solely subscriber additions to an account-economics model, focusing on deeper customer relationships and higher revenue per account. The company is extracting more revenue per billing relationship, as shown by rising ARPA and service revenue growth, largely supported by expanding broadband services. While the model is durable due to consistent free cash flow generation, investors should monitor churn, leverage, and pricing discipline to assess its long-term resilience.
articles.content: T-Mobile's growth is shifting from solely subscriber additions to an account-economics model, focusing on deeper customer relationships and higher revenue per account. The company is extracting more revenue per billing relationship, as shown by rising ARPA and service revenue growth, largely supported by expanding broadband services. While the model is durable due to consistent free cash flow generation, investors should monitor churn, leverage, and pricing discipline to assess its long-term resilience.

articles.title: Verisk Analytics : Memorial Day Cargo Theft Hit Five-Year High in 2025, Verisk CargoNet Warns of Selective Targeting in 2026
articles.summary: Verisk CargoNet is cautioning businesses about increased cargo theft risks during the Memorial Day holiday period in 2026, following a five-year high in incidents in 2025. Organized theft groups are employing more selective, high-value targeting, including sophisticated fraud schemes beyond traditional trailer theft. Companies are advised to implement heightened security measures, verify delivery changes, and maintain shipment visibility to mitigate risks.
articles.content: Verisk CargoNet is cautioning businesses about increased cargo theft risks during the Memorial Day holiday period in 2026, following a five-year high in incidents in 2025. Organized theft groups are employing more selective, high-value targeting, including sophisticated fraud schemes beyond traditional trailer theft. Companies are advised to implement heightened security measures, verify delivery changes, and maintain shipment visibility to mitigate risks.

articles.summary: Target is continuing to expand its "Food Forward" strategy by focusing on high-growth food and beverage areas, including protein, functional drinks, and better-for-you snacking. The company introduced 3,000 new food items in Q1 2026, with sales from these items growing over 50%. Despite a strong first quarter with net sales up 7% to $25.4 billion, Target acknowledges declining consumer sentiment and plans for cautious growth.

articles.title: Applied Materials 30% Systems Outlook Is the Headline. The Services Business May Be the Better Story.
articles.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) posted record revenue in Q2 2026, raising its semiconductor equipment growth outlook to over 30%. While the equipment segment receives significant attention, the company's Applied Global Services (AGS) business is emerging as a strong performer, with growth targets upgraded to mid-teens annual CAGR. AGS, comprising long-term service contracts, on-demand parts, and sophisticated factory software, is driven by increasing demand for fab optimization, particularly from AI-focused customers, and generates higher operating margins than the equipment segment.

The company recently exceeded Q1 earnings expectations, reporting an EPS of $1.55 and revenue of $1.24 billion, and subsequently raised its FY 2027 and Q2 2027 guidance.
